title: anthyllis vulneraria subsp. sampaioana
intro: Anthyllis vulneraria subsp. sampaioana, also known as sanguisorba sampaioana or Sampaio's kidney vetch, is a subspecies of the kidney vetch plant, a flowering plant in the legume family that has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties. It is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for wound healing, skin conditions, and digestive issues. The plant's health benefits include reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and exhibiting antimicrobial activity, which may help prevent infections. The herbal preparations of Anthyllis vulneraria subsp. sampaioana can be found in topical creams and ointments, as well as in teas and infusions, although its use should be consulted with a healthcare professional due to potential interactions with other medications.
